assignments.name: ocr_text
assignments.value:
Speiseplan KW 30
Mo: Nudeln mit Tomatensauce
Di: Kartoffelsuppe
Aushang vom Elternbeirat. Bei Fragen: Frau Meier, Tel. 0151 23456789, anna.meier@example.de
Max Mustermann, geb. 12.03.2020, bringt Kuchen mit.

nodes.name: Mock OCR Text (raw, from worker)
nodes.notes: Stands in for the Tesseract OCR output INSIDE the worker. This raw text contains PII (a name, phone, email, a child's name + birthdate). It must NEVER leave the box un-redacted. In the MVP this string never exists outside the VPS.

nodes.name: Local Redaction (no AI)
nodes.notes: THE PRIVACY BOUNDARY. Runs entirely locally — no external call. Masks PII to [TEL_1], [MAIL_1], [NAME_1], … and forwards ONLY the redacted text. In the MVP this is Presidio + spaCy + a German regex pack, fail-closed (worker/redaction.py).

nodes.name: Boundary Assert (fail-closed)
nodes.notes: Belt-and-braces: re-checks the redacted text for any surviving PII pattern. boundary_ok=false would stop the pipeline (fail-closed). This makes the privacy invariant VISIBLE: only redacted text proceeds.

nodes.name: Boundary OK?
nodes.notes: TRUE = redacted text is clean -> proceed to the LLM (see POC 2). FALSE = PII survived -> block (fail-closed). On the default mock data this is TRUE: all PII was masked.

nodes.name: → Send REDACTED text to LLM
nodes.notes: This is the ONLY text that ever crosses to the external LLM (Anthropic). No raw image, no un-redacted PII. Continues in POC 2.

nodes.name: BLOCK (fail-closed, no external call)
nodes.notes: If redaction somehow left PII, nothing is sent externally. The MVP routes such a post to the manual path instead.
